message:
feat(approval): Cx-first issue_for_plan (plan-bound token) (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 59. Adds the plan-bound variant of Cx-first
token issue. Parallel to tick-59's `issue_with_cx` but includes
the plan_hash/plan_version/risk_summary binding fields needed for
TOCTOU-safe approval flows where the plan is rehashed between
issue and consume.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ApprovalStore::issue_for_plan_with_cx(cx, input, plan_hash,
    plan_version, risk_summary)` — Cx-first parallel of
    `issue_for_plan`. Same cancellation-safety structure as
    tick-59: pre-flight checkpoint + between-count-and-insert
    checkpoint. Token record is written to storage AFTER both
    checkpoints, so cancel during either window leaves no
    partial state.

Plan-binding semantics identical to legacy — the written
`ApprovalTokenRecord` carries:
  * `plan_hash: Some(plan_hash.to_string())`
  * `plan_version: plan_version` (the caller-supplied Option)
  * `risk_summary: risk_summary.clone()` (also carried into the
    returned `ApprovalRequest.summary`)

Regression test (new)
`issue_for_plan_with_cx_preserves_plan_binding`:

  * Uses TWO distinct Cx-issued tokens to exercise both the
    matching-plan_hash success path AND the mismatched-plan_hash
    rejection path cleanly. Single-token tests can't cover both
    because the legacy `consume_for_plan` is TOCTOU-safe: a
    mismatched plan_hash still CONSUMES the token (invalidating
    it against reuse with the correct hash).

  * Token 1: matching plan_hash must consume; consumed record
    carries the expected `plan_hash`, `plan_version=Some(7)`,
    `risk_summary="Cx-first plan binding"`.

  * Token 2: mismatched plan_hash must return None. Second
    attempt with the correct plan_hash also returns None,
    proving the TOCTOU protection (token exhausted after first
    presentation regardless of hash validity).

The TOCTOU retry check catches a subtle regression class: if a
future refactor of `issue_for_plan_with_cx` wrote the token with
`plan_hash: None` (losing the binding), the mismatched consume
would INCORRECTLY succeed — the test would surface that drift.

Cx-first approval.rs progress:
  * `issue`                          ✓ (tick 59)
  * `issue_for_plan`                 ✓ (tick 60, this commit)
  * `consume`                        — pending
  * `consume_with_context`           — pending
  * `consume_for_plan`               — pending
  * `consume_for_plan_with_context`  — pending
  * `attach_to_decision`             — pending

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * approval:: — 96 passed / 0 failed (tick 59: 95 -> tick 60: 96,
    +1 new parity + TOCTOU test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
